description: Baseline measurement: 34 redis commands per lock+unlock cycle
batch UNLINK queued+primed in lock.lua (marginal, -1 per single cycle)
defer LLEN/HLEN in unlock.lua, batch UNLINK calls (-14.7%)
defer LLEN/HLEN in queue.lua to limit-check branch (-7.4%)
SET NX in queue.lua to avoid GET+SET on first enqueue (-3.7%)
remove redundant locked? pre-check before unlock (-3.8%)
DEAD END: remove locked? in lock! - broke re-lock semantics (3 failures)
skip LREM queued/primed in unlock when job holds lock (-8%)
skip LREM in lock.lua for single-lock case, just UNLINK (-8.7%)
fast-path unlock for single-lock, batch all UNLINK (-9.5%)
UNLINK in lock.lua duplicate check for limit<=1 (correctness)
merge queue+lock into single Lua script for non-blocking path (-31.6%)
skip Ruby-side locked? check for sync lock path
skip sentinel LPUSH+PEXPIRE in unlock for sync-locked single locks (-15.4%)
fast-path unlock for sync-locked single locks, skip HEXISTS (-9.1%)
use HLEN-first in queue_and_lock.lua for limit=1, skip HEXISTS (-10%)
extend sync fast path in unlock for until_expired (until_expired 13→10)
skip redundant HDEL before UNLINK(locked) for sync single-lock (-11.1%)
simplify SET NX to unconditional SET (code cleanup, no metric change)
